front.title: VEIL: Verified Ephemeral Identity Layer for OAuth 2.1

abstract.t: VEIL (Verified Ephemeral Identity Layer) is a security profile of OAuth 2.1 for privacy-preserving identity verification. It separates claims into two tracks: proof claims (boolean verification results, compliance flags, assurance levels) travel through standard token claims, while identity claims (name, date of birth, address, nationality) travel only through an ephemeral, single-consume channel that keeps personally identifiable information off long-lived tokens. Subject identifiers are pairwise by default. Consent records are HMAC-protected. Step-up authentication scales with operation sensitivity. VEIL is the base profile for domain-specific extensions.
